title: Fi expands Starlink direct-to-device capability into dog-tracking
summary: Fi launched a dog tracker using T-Mobile’s Starlink-enabled T-Satellite service for nationwide connectivity. The device works even outside terrestrial networks.

title: The satellite industry’s most expensive problem isn’t in orbit
summary: AST SpaceMobile deployed three additional BlueBird satellites into low Earth orbit, aiming to build a space-based cellular broadband network of 45 to 60 satellites. The company is advancing its plans despite industry challenges.

title: Venus Aerospace raises $91 million to scale rocket engine technology
summary: Venus Aerospace has secured $91 million in funding to advance its rotating detonation rocket engine technology. The investment will support the development and production of the innovative propulsion system.

title: The government’s options to address strained spaceports
summary: The explosion of a Blue Origin New Glenn rocket at Cape Canaveral's Launch Complex 36 caused significant damage and highlighted the need for government intervention to address strained spaceports. This incident underscores the challenges of managing and maintaining aging or overburdened launch infrastructure.

title: Space Force adds Relativity, Impulse Space to national security launch program
summary: The U.S. Space Force has included Relativity Space and Impulse Space in its NSSL Phase 3 Lane 1 program, expanding competition for national security launch missions. This move allows newer companies to bid on military space missions.

title: Skyroot prepares for first orbital launch attempt
summary: Skyroot Aerospace, an Indian launch startup, is preparing for its first orbital launch attempt as soon as July 12. The company aims to scale up to monthly launches following this milestone.

title: D-Orbit signs launch-services contract with ArkEdge Space
summary: D-Orbit has signed a launch-services contract with Japanese startup ArkEdge Space to provide launches using its ION Satellite Carrier. The agreement was announced on July 8.

title: Orbit Fab hires new CEO and raises funding to support satellite refueling business
summary: Orbit Fab has appointed a new CEO and secured additional funding to advance its satellite refueling operations. The company aims to transition from technology development to commercial services.

title: Spirit Electronics Announces Managed Access to U.S.-Based Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing for Aerospace and Defense Programs
summary: Spirit Electronics launched a managed-access program to provide U.S.-based advanced semiconductor manufacturing for aerospace and defense customers. This initiative ensures secure access to cutting-edge chip production for critical programs.

title: Apolink makes contact with first relay satellite
summary: Apolink successfully established contact with its first relay satellite launched via SpaceX's July 7 rideshare mission. This milestone enables a data relay demonstration under a novel experimental FCC license.

title: The US Space Force just got a new electromagnetic weapon to jam adversary satellites
summary: The U.S. Space Force has acquired its first publicly acknowledged offensive weapon, capable of jamming adversary satellites with electromagnetic radiation. This marks a significant step in space warfare capabilities.

title: Revolutionary rocket engine company Venus Aerospace raises $91 million to scale design
summary: Venus Aerospace has raised $91 million to advance its rotating detonation rocket engine technology, aiming to transition from testing to production for various applications.

title: NASA's New Horizons probe just woke up from hibernation 6 billion miles away beyond Pluto. What's it doing out there?
summary: NASA's New Horizons probe woke up from hibernation 6 billion miles away beyond Pluto. The spacecraft is in good health after nearly a year in sleep mode.

title: James Webb Space Telescope celebrates its 4th birthday with stunning image of a galaxy crash site
summary: NASA released a stunning image of the galaxy Centaurus A to celebrate the James Webb Space Telescope's 4th birthday. The image depicts a cosmic collision site from 2 billion years ago.

title: Our Milky Way galaxy might be larger than we thought
summary: Astronomers discovered that the Milky Way galaxy might be larger than previously estimated by analyzing cosmic explosions. This challenges existing assumptions about the galaxy's size.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' is finally streaming Amazon Prime Video, and we can't wait to watch it again
summary: The sci-fi movie 'Project Hail Mary' is now available on Amazon Prime Video starting July 3. Fans are excited to rewatch the space-themed film.

title: Artemis 2's Jeremy Hansen stepping down from active astronaut duty after epic moon mission
summary: Jeremy Hansen, the Canadian astronaut assigned to NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission, will step down from active astronaut duty in September to take on a new role. This marks a significant transition for the Canadian Space Agency following his historic lunar mission assignment.

title: How public–private partnerships can turn Mars into an economic frontier (Op-Ed)
summary: A public–private partnership is essential for developing technologies and systems required for sustained Mars settlement, as no single agency can achieve this alone.

title: China releases 1st photo of Earth's elusive 'quasi-moon' Kamo'oalewa
summary: China's Tianwen-2 probe captured the first photo of Earth's quasi-moon Kamo'oalewa, also known as asteroid 2016 HO3. This marks a significant milestone in China's space exploration efforts.

title: Cottonwood Fire Chars Utah
summary: The Cottonwood Fire burned over 150 square miles in Utah, impacting areas of a ski resort. The wildfire caused significant damage and required extensive firefighting efforts.

title: NASA Transfers ‘Hundred Acre Wood’ to Patuxent Research Refuge
summary: NASA transferred ownership of 105 acres of wooded land at its Goddard Space Flight Center to the Patuxent Research Refuge, managed by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service. The land, formerly known as Area 400, is now part of the largest unfragmented forest block in the region.

title: NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health
summary: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft has woken from its longest hibernation period of nearly a year in good health. It is now ready to transmit science data from the distant Kuiper Belt beyond Pluto.

title: Artemis II Crew and Apollo 14 Moon Tree
summary: The Artemis II crew participated in the dedication of an Apollo 14 Moon tree at NASA's Johnson Space Center. The tree is a second-generation loblolly pine species grown from seeds carried aboard the Apollo 14 mission.

title: July 2026 Satellite Puzzler
summary: The July 2026 Satellite Puzzler challenges participants to identify the location of a satellite image and explain its significance. The image is notable for its unique geographical or environmental features.

title: The World Cup From 250 Miles Up
summary: Astronauts on the International Space Station have photographed cities hosting the 2026 FIFA World Cup from 250 miles above Earth. These images highlight the unique perspective of space-based observations of major global events.

title: NASA Takes Flight For America’s 250th
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman leads a flyover in his personally owned Northrop F-5 Tiger aircraft during the Great American State Fair on July 4, 2026, on the National Mall in Washington, D.C. The event celebrates America's 250th anniversary and highlights the nation's history of exploration and innovation.

title: NASA Seeks Industry Input on Second Phase of Commercial Space Stations
summary: NASA released a draft RFP seeking industry feedback for the next phase of its commercial space stations strategy to transition low Earth orbit activities from the ISS. The move aims to ensure continuity in space operations as the ISS nears retirement.

title: NASA’s CAPSTONE Completes Extended Mission Testing Lunar Technologies
summary: NASA's CAPSTONE mission has completed an extended mission to test lunar technologies, validating autonomous navigation and communication systems for future Moon missions. The spacecraft was designed to operate without a direct connection to Earth, supporting NASA's goal of a sustained human presence on the Moon.

title: NASA Webb Uncovers Unusual Galaxy Shaped by Cosmic Collision
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope reveals a complex galaxy, Centaurus A, transformed by cosmic collision in new infrared images. The unprecedented sensitivity uncovers details previously obscured by dust.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being developed for Stoke Space and Relativity Space, contributing to the region's revitalization. Multiple launch providers are advancing their facilities at Cape Canaveral.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved multiple milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital outpost.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar missions and establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent New Glenn anomaly that caused significant damage. The company is working to restore the facility to operational status.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: NASASpaceFlight.com previews two upcoming space missions: a rescue operation for the Swift observatory and the final launch of an Atlas satellite. The article highlights scheduled orbital launches this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to extend the lifespan of a NASA space telescope. This mission marks the first flight of the Pegasus XL since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, providing data that will aid future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The image highlights Euclid's capability to study the cosmos in high detail.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry news, but the specific content was not provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the organization's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The art of hadrons
summary: The news discusses 'The art of hadrons,' but no further details are provided in the given text.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25). These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm technology designed to be versatile and applicable across multiple space missions. This innovation could significantly enhance robotic operations in space exploration and satellite servicing.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas for mission success.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' is vague and lacks context. It may refer to a satellite or spacecraft component designed for durability and precision.

title: ispace Launches Lunar Integrator Service With Starship
summary: ispace has secured 500 kg of payload capacity on SpaceX's Starship for a lunar mission launching no earlier than 2030. This initiative marks ispace's entry into the Lunar Integrator Service, expanding its lunar logistics capabilities.

title: Venus Aerospace Raises $91M Series B to Scale RDRE Production
summary: Venus Aerospace has raised $91 million in a Series B funding round to expand the production of its Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine (RDRE) technology. This investment highlights the growing industry interest in RDRE, a more efficient propulsion method.

title: FCC Poised To Advance Sat Licensing Reform
summary: The FCC is set to vote on the Space Modernization Order on July 22, aiming to reform satellite licensing processes. FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced the upcoming decision, which could modernize regulations in the space industry.

title: Transporter-17 Sends the First Commercial Nuclear Sat to Orbit
summary: SpaceX's Transporter-17 rideshare mission launched 81 payloads to LEO, including the first commercial nuclear satellite. The mission lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Base at 3:12 AM.

title: NASA Releases CLD Draft RFP
summary: NASA has released a draft Request for Proposal (RFP) for Commercial Low Earth Orbit (CLD) stand-alone replacements to the ISS, following industry validation of human habitation in LEO. This marks a key step in NASA's plan to transition from the ISS to commercial space stations.

title: Exclusive: Ravee Optics Raises $6M Seed to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics has raised $6 million in a seed funding round to develop compact laser communication terminals aimed at making laser comms more accessible. The company's terminals are designed to be lighter, smaller, and easier to manufacture at scale.

title: The Biggest Fundraises of H1 2026
summary: The article discusses the largest fundraises in the space industry during the first half of 2026, highlighting how increased investment benefits rocket launches and payload deployments globally.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US have fostered stronger space industry collaborations with Middle Eastern companies. This shift highlights new opportunities for partnerships in the global space sector.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly delivered imagery of a Rocket Lab spacecraft within 61 hours of receiving an order from SSC, beating the 72-hour deadline. The rapid response highlights True Anomaly's efficiency in space operations.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space to develop its second military satellite, Aurora. The mission is part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: CesiumAstro Files for 737-Satellite Synchronicity Constellation
summary: CesiumAstro filed an FCC application for a 737-satellite constellation named Synchronicity to offer reconfigurable connectivity for fixed and mobile users. The Austin, Texas-based company specializes in software-defined radio technology.

title: NATO Allies Plan Hybrid HALO Constellation
summary: Eight NATO allies plan to integrate national military satellites into a hybrid constellation named HALO to enhance NATO's space resilience and military advantage. The initiative aims to improve collective defense capabilities through shared satellite resources.

title: SpaceX’s Transporter-17 Mission Launches 81 Payloads
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 81 payloads on its Transporter-17 rideshare mission aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base. The mission marks another milestone in SpaceX's commercial rideshare program.

title: Space Systems Command Awards SES 5-Year Blanket Purchase Agreement
summary: The U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command awarded SES Space and Defense a five-year blanket purchase agreement for global Ku-band connectivity services. The contract includes managed connectivity solutions.

title: An Expedition Across the Arctic-Space Connection With David Marsh
summary: David Marsh recently returned from an expedition to the Arctic to study parallels between Arctic conditions and space environments. The findings will be discussed in an upcoming episode of 'On Orbit'.

title: Neo Space and Hughes Deploy 3 Ground Gateways to Support IFC
summary: Neo Space Group and Hughes Network Systems have deployed three new Hughes ground gateways in Saudi Arabia and Europe to support NSG's in-flight connectivity (IFC). This deployment aims to enhance NSG's satellite-based connectivity services for airlines.
company: Neo Space Group and Hughes Network Systems

title: Univity Signs MoU With Indonesia’s Telkomsat on VLEO and D2D Connectivity
summary: Univity has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Indonesia's Telkomsat to collaborate on satellite connectivity, including assessing Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) architecture and direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity.

title: Mission to Rescue NASA Swift Observatory Launches
summary: A mission to rescue NASA's Swift Observatory has launched, with the Katalyst Space LINK servicing spacecraft successfully contacted. Northrop Grumman executed the launch.

title: Cyber in Space Signs Partnership Deal with ExeQuantum
summary: Cyber in Space has partnered with ExeQuantum to enhance cyber resilience and quantum-readiness services for the global aerospace and space ecosystem. The collaboration aims to deliver advanced security solutions to organizations in the industry.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's Leo constellation has achieved enough satellites in orbit to support initial service after a ULA launch. The mission marks a key milestone for Amazon's satellite internet ambitions.
